title: Cisco ISE External Identity Store Health (AD/LDAP/RADIUS/SAML/OAuth)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(j) covers MFA / continuous-auth solutions. Identity store health is foundational; without it, MFA cannot operate.
Art.9 (Protection and prevention) requires ICT system robustness. Identity-store availability is a foundational input.
compliance.controlObjective: ICT system robustness for identity flows.

compliance.assurance_rationale: IA-5 (Authenticator Management) — identity-store health evidences operational availability of the authentication factor mechanism.
A.5.18 (Access rights) — operational availability of the identity-store evidences continuous access-rights enforcement.
CC6.6 — logical access. Identity-store health is foundational.

controlTest.positiveScenario: On a lab AD, stop the LDAP service. Within 5 min ISE emits 24014. UC-17.1.44 fires showing AD store with non-zero count.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Restart LDAP service. ISE re-establishes connection. Alert clears.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Identity Store* and *Authentication* categories. Codes per store type — Active Directory: `24014` (AD connection lost), `24015` (AD authentication latency), `24023` (AD machine account password rotation failed), `24033` (AD trust relationship broken), `24050` (AD LDAP referral failed). LDAP: `24400`/`24401`/`24402` (LDAP search failure / timeout / bind failure). External RADIUS proxy: `11051` (proxy timeout), `11052` (proxy server unreachable). SAML/OAuth identity providers: `82400` (SAML/OAuth IdP connection lost), `82401` (SAML/OAuth metadata refresh failed). ISE OpenAPI `/api/v1/identitystore/healthcheck` polled every 5 min for active health stats per store, written to `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:idstore_health`.
spl:
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (24014, 24015, 24023, 24033, 24050, 24400, 24401, 24402, 11051, 11052, 82400, 82401))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:idstore_health")
| eval store_type = case(
    MessageCode IN (24014, 24015, 24023, 24033, 24050), "active_directory",
    MessageCode IN (24400, 24401, 24402), "ldap",
    MessageCode IN (11051, 11052), "external_radius",
    MessageCode IN (82400, 82401), "saml_oauth",
    isnotnull(store_name), "snapshot")
| stats count latest(_time) as last_event, latest(health_state) as health by store_name, store_type
| where count > 0 OR health=="DOWN"
| sort - count
description: Detects external identity store outages, search/bind failures, and degraded performance — covers Active Directory, LDAP, external RADIUS proxy, and SAML/OAuth identity providers used by ISE. When the upstream identity store is unhealthy, *every authentication that depends on it* fails or runs slowly.
value: ISE is a federation point — it doesn't own the identity, it queries upstream stores. AD outage means no employee can authenticate. LDAP timeout means slow auth, NAD timeouts, dropped sessions. SAML/OAuth IdP outage breaks the cloud-app SSO flow. Catching upstream identity-store issues at first occurrence allows the platform team to engage AD/IDP teams immediately rather than waiting for user-impact reports.
implementation: Confirm *Identity Store*, *Authentication*, and *Failed Attempts* logging categories are at INFO. Deploy the OpenAPI health-check poller. Schedule the search every 5 min, search window `-15m@m to @m`, trigger on count > 0, throttle suppression on `store_name` for 30 min.
visualization: (1) Single value: stores in DOWN state. (2) Per-store latency timechart, last 24h. (3) Per-store error-rate timechart, last 24h. (4) Top-N: stores with most failures last 7d.
dataModelAcceleration: Yes — Authentication DM acceleration helps cross-correlation with auth-failure UCs.

references.title: Cisco ISE 3.4 Admin Guide — Identity Stores
Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services (Splunkbase 1915)

knownFalsePositives: (a) **AD machine-account password rotation** — Periodic rotation may briefly trigger 24023 events; recovery is automatic. Suppress isolated single events. (b) **AD scheduled maintenance** — Cross-reference change calendar; downgrade to notification within window. (c) **LDAP referral following** — Some directories use referrals heavily; the 24050 event isn't a failure if the referral resolves. Tune severity per store. (d) **SAML metadata refresh blip** — Metadata endpoints may transiently 502; alert only when 82401 recurs >3 times in 1h.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• ISE *Identity Store* and *Authentication* categories at INFO.
• `id_stores.csv` lookup with `store_name`, `store_type`, `is_critical`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Validate:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (24014, 24400, 11051, 82400) earliest=-7d
| stats count by MessageCode
```

Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 5 min.

Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as in controlTest.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — Identity Store Health":
• Per-store status tile.
• Latency timechart.
• Error-rate timechart.
• Top-N stores with failures.

Alerting: PagerDuty high on critical-store DOWN. Auto-route to AD team / LDAP team / IDP team based on `store_type`.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **All AD events show one DC** — Single DC configured; add additional DCs for redundancy.
• **LDAP events constant** — TLS cert mismatch on LDAP server or insufficient privileges for ISE bind account.
• **SAML metadata fails repeatedly** — IdP changed metadata URL; update in ISE config.
grandmaExplanation: ISE asks other systems (like the company directory) whether someone is allowed to log in. We watch those other systems too — and ring the bell when ISE can't reach the directory or when the directory is slow to answer.
